Name: A map of trails in parks or properties under the jurisdiction of the department of parks and recreation.
Title: A Local Law to amend the administrative code of the city of New York, in relation to a map of trails in parks or properties under the jurisdiction of the department of parks and recreation

History.Description: This Introduction was Introduced by Council
This Introduction was Referred to Comm by Council to the Committee on Parks and Recreation
This Introduction was Hearing Held by Committee
This Introduction was Laid Over by Committee

Attachments.Name: Proposed Int. No. 577-A - Fiscal Impact Statement - City Council

Summary: This bill would require the Department of Parks and Recreation (DPR) to post on its website a map of trails located in each park or property under DPR jurisdiction. DPR would be required to update the map upon the completion of any project that improves an existing trail or creates a new one and would have to consider how any project that improves or creates a trail would improve public access to that trail.
Text:
Be it enacted by the Council as follows:
      
      
      Section 1. Chapter 1 of title 18 of the administrative code of the city of New York is amended by adding a new section 18-170 to read as follows: 
      � 18-170 Trails. a. Definition. For purposes of this section, the term "trail" means a path within a natural area that is reserved for recreational activities, such as walking or bicycling, and on which the use of motor vehicles is prohibited.  
      b. The commissioner shall post on the department's website a map of trails in each park or property under the jurisdiction of the department. The department shall update such map upon completion of any project to improve a trail or to create a new trail. 
      c. The commissioner shall consider how any project that improves a trail or creates a new trail could improve public access to such trail. 
      � 2. This local law takes effect immediately.
